chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يُجْزِئُ مِنْ حَجَّةِ الْإِسْلَامِ وَ مَا لَا يُجْزِئُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 38 – What suffices from the Hajj of Al-Islam and what does not suffice

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/166)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/166)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَمُوتُ وَ لَمْ يَحُجَّ حَجَّةَ الْإِسْلَامِ وَ لَمْ يُوصِ بِهَا أَ يُقْضَى عَنْهُ قَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Fazalat, from Rafa’at who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about a man who dies and did not perform Hajj, a Hajj of Al-Islam, and did not bequeath with it, does it have to be fulfilled on his behalf?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 15. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Fadalah from Rifa’ah who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who dies and has not performed Hajjahta al-Islam and has not made any will about it if it can be carried out on his behalf. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, it is permissible.’”
